judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are very similar in substance and hit a, b, and c equally well; Report 1 additionally identifies the missing environment/global-level gate and recommends adding one (expectation d), which Report 2 omits, making Report 1 more complete overall.

report_1.evidence: Line 11-12 is the exact decision point: when "auto_send_reminders" is absent from tenant.settings (or explicitly null), .get() returns None, and the function returns True.

report_2.evidence: Line 11-12 is the deciding logic: if the tenant's JSONB settings dict has no "auto_send_reminders" key (i.e., the clinic admin has never touched the toggle), .get() returns None, and the function returns True.

report_1.evidence: I would change auto_send_reminders_enabled (slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13) so that None/missing returns False instead of True

report_2.evidence: I would change the missing-value branch in auto_send_reminders_enabled (backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-12) to return False instead of True, so the feature is opt-in per clinic

report_1.evidence: Contrast this with compact_calendar_view_enabled at feature_flags.py:4-7, which gates a UI display preference — worst case of a wrong default there is a mildly annoying calendar layout, not a message a patient didn't expect to receive.

report_2.evidence: Compare with compact_calendar_view_enabled (backend/app/feature_flags.py:4-7), which defaults to True for missing settings too — but that only affects the admin's own calendar layout. Nobody outside the clinic is affected, and it's trivially reversible. The two features do not share a risk profile, so they should not share a default.

report_1.evidence: Confirm there's no env-level master switch. project documentation's recipe says a per-tenant flag should be ANDed with an env-level master gate. I found no such master switch anywhere in this repo (infra/main.tf only defines an environment variable) — decide whether one is needed before flipping any tenant to "on" in production, so there's a single kill switch independent of per-tenant data.
